recipe.title: Fish Crocchette Appetizer
recipe.instructions:
1. Begin to cook the fish and potatoes.
  2. Cook cod in a saucepan.
  3. Boil the potatoes in their skins in a pot with the water or in the microwave in a container with two inches of water (for the cooking time in the microwave, read the instruction booklet)
  4. Let cool cod, eliminated any remaining bones. In a bowl add the mashed potatoes, the minced cod, the garlic cooked in the milk, tuna, drained, Parmesan, chopped parsley, salt , egg and the half lemon zest grated.
  5. Mix well the mixture , form into balls to the same size if possible.
  6. Coat in white flour, dip in beaten egg and into bread crumbs, fry in canola oil (325 degrees F) until golden , dry on paper towels.
recipe.summary: If you have around 45 minutes to spend in the kitchen, Fish Crocchette Appetizer might be a spectacular pescatarian recipe to try. For $2.22 per serving, you get a main course that serves 8. One serving contains 406 calories, 27g of protein, and 21g of fat. This recipe from Foodista has 2 fans. If you have bread crumbs, canolan oil, cod fillet, and a few other ingredients on hand, you can make it. With a spoonacular score of 70%, this dish is pretty good. Try Homemade Fish Shaped Appetizer, Gefilte Fish Appetizer (Doctored from the Jar), and Crocchette di miglio, carote e olive nere for similar recipes.
ingredients: 500 grams cod fillet (or any white fish fillet)
